incidentReport.title: Complete API Gateway Outage — US-East Region

rootCause.summary: A misconfigured rate limiting rule deployed at 14:18 UTC set the global request threshold to 100 req/s instead of 100,000 req/s. The configuration change was pushed through the fast-track deployment pipeline which bypasses staging validation.
rootCause.triggerEvent: Deployment of rate-limiter v2.4.1 via fast-track pipeline
contributingFactors: Fast-track pipeline lacks configuration validation step
Rate limit value was stored as a raw integer without unit suffix, making 100 vs 100000 visually ambiguous in the config file
No automatic rollback trigger — the deployment health check only verified the service was running, not that it was processing traffic correctly
The on-call engineer was in a meeting and initially missed the PagerDuty alert (3-minute acknowledge time is within SLA but the alert escalation path was suboptimal)

responseActions.action: Automated monitoring detected error rate spike (>50% 429 responses)
On-call engineer Alex Kim acknowledged alert and began investigation
Identified rate limiter as potential cause. Attempted to increase limits via admin panel — access denied due to recent RBAC changes
Escalated to platform team lead. Sarah Okafor joined incident channel
Confirmed root cause: rate-limiter v2.4.1 config. Attempted rollback but fast-track pipeline does not support one-click rollback
Manually deployed rate-limiter v2.4.0 through standard pipeline
Traffic restored. Error rates returned to baseline. Monitoring for stability
All queued webhook deliveries processed. Incident declared resolved

immediate: Sent customer communication acknowledging outage and SLA credits

shortTerm.action: Add configuration validation to fast-track pipeline
Implement automatic rollback on error rate threshold breach
Add unit suffix to all rate limit configuration values (e.g., '100000/s' instead of '100000')

longTerm.action: Implement canary deployments for all configuration changes
Build configuration diff review requirement for production changes

lessonsLearned: Fast-track deployment pipelines must have the same safety checks as standard pipelines — speed should not bypass validation
Configuration values should be self-documenting (include units, ranges, and descriptions)
Health checks must verify functional correctness, not just process liveness
RBAC changes should be communicated to on-call engineers before they go live
incidentReport.author: Sarah Okafor
